title: Finest Episodes
wikitext:
[]: Finest Episodes?             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Wed Dec 20, 2006 8:38 am I think we can all agree that some episodes of Evangelion are stronger than others, and people here probably favor certain ones over others, so, what episodes do you like the most among the bunch of them? I think a lot of people here will agree that Episode 19 is one of the best episodes in the entire series, not only is the battle against Zeruel EPIC, but it also drives the plot forward A LOT and marks when the series takes a turn for the really dark, Episodes 16 through 18 were dark aswell, however, I consider Episode 19 the point in which you're completely assured that all the lighthearted-ness of the Action Arc is completely over.

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Wed Dec 20, 2006 10:20 am Yes But to some extent, that shift really is in episode 16. Maybe even 15? You know; from which point every angel hereon out actually manages to do some LASTING DAMAGE, and not just be forgotten right away.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Dec 20, 2006 3:08 pm In hindsight, 15 is the gathering storm; but 16 is definitely when you suddenly realise you're all in this handcart together. 16 also has a special place for me, as it's the episode where Asuka stole my heart.

[]:             Quote Post by master0rolando » Wed Dec 20, 2006 10:03 pm best episode is 11... lots of character development...its great to see the three childeren interactiong with each other like that...a lot of anime has a little too much of that...but its rarer in Eva...plenty of humor...a creative problem...a creative enemy...and creative way of beating the enemy...and a happy ending... i love that epsiode

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Wed Dec 20, 2006 10:13 pm Matariel was kind of pathetic, but it's one of the few times we see Asuka interact with Rei in any other way than "I hate you you stupid fucking doll*Bitchslap*"

[]:             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Wed Dec 20, 2006 11:05 pm 24. Absolutely no contest.

[]:             Quote Post by Big Panda » Thu Dec 21, 2006 1:03 am master0rolando wrote: best episode is 11... lots of character development...its great to see the three childeren interactiong with each other like that...a lot of anime has a little too much of that...but its rarer in Eva...plenty of humor...a creative problem...a creative enemy...and creative way of beating the enemy...and a happy ending... i love that epsiode

[]:             Quote Post by Space Penis » Thu Dec 21, 2006 7:39 pm #6, #19 and #23, definately. Rei isn't a particulary easy character to relate to, but the bits with her in agony in the entry plug in #23 are heartwrenching. Same for #6 really. She really shines in those two episodes.

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Thu Dec 21, 2006 8:41 pm Trigger's Elysium wrote: 24. Absolutely no contest.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Dec 22, 2006 6:57 pm MaggotMaster wrote: Trigger's Elysium wrote: 24. Absolutely no contest.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Fri Dec 22, 2006 7:14 pm Ornette wrote: MaggotMaster wrote: Trigger's Elysium wrote: 24. Absolutely no contest.

[]:             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Fri Dec 22, 2006 9:21 pm MaggotMaster wrote: I would like that episode better if the NPC footage didn't turn it into the biggest plothole in Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Dec 23, 2006 5:28 pm I guess this doesn't mean favorite? In that case, I personally feel finest would seriously have to go to both 25 & 26 (not gonna include EoE as it's more of a movie than an episode.) for various reasons: 1) Digging into the characters psychic. 2) The artistic way it was done. Flashing text and images works for me. 3) The fact Anno was able to pull off both EoTV and EoE being concurrent. 4) Overall theme of learning to love yourself so you can learn others & life is what you make of it as well as a matter of perspective. My favorite episodes, however, would have to be: ~22', because of seeing how/why Asuka ended up the way she did, I also felt the characterization was great. ~8 because of the nonstop-comedy in the episode (rare for Eva!) as well as Asuka's appearance. ~4 gives me fuzzies for some reason, probably because of Shinji's depression ; the scene of Shinji sitting alone in the train listening to track 26(? Aoi Legend) still gives me chills; HOWEVER, I seriously do like all of the episodes for some reason or another.

[]:             Quote Post by Jobhug » Sat Dec 23, 2006 7:30 pm For me episode 23, for a bunch of reasons. Asuka saying something along the lines of "Am I so worthless he couldn't do that [Sortie Unit 01] for me?" has always stuck with me as one of the msot painful moments in Evangelion. Then it's surpassed like 15 minutes later when the tank full of Reis all look up. And then seeing Ritsuko hunched over crying, after being such a strong, detatched character is distressing. Therefore 23 gets my award for finest episode. It makes me feel a range of emotions ranging from Horror to Disgust. Although episode 21 is my favourite (I'm a fan of Fuyutsuki's character)

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Sat Dec 23, 2006 10:20 pm In before Jet Alone LOL JUST KIDDING

[]:             Quote Post by Mundane » Sat Dec 23, 2006 11:09 pm Episode 18. I just love watching heads explode, personal preference really.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Dec 23, 2006 11:20 pm Mundane wrote: Episode 18. I just love watching heads explode, personal preference really.

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Sat Dec 23, 2006 11:42 pm Ornette wrote: Mundane wrote: Episode 18. I just love watching heads explode, personal preference really.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Dec 24, 2006 11:26 am To quote Bart & Milhouse watching South Park : "Cartoon Violence! Cartoon violence!" I usually hate violence (I'm like Maya in that respect, lol), but I was acutally getting into watching that one. :shock: Watching Angels being eaten is fun, too. :twisted:

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Dec 24, 2006 1:02 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: To quote Bart & Milhouse watching South Park : "Cartoon Violence! Cartoon violence!" I usually hate violence (I'm like Maya in that respect, lol), but I was acutally getting into watching that one. :shock: Watching Angels being eaten is fun, too. :twisted:
